Ad Text:
Hybrid work environment

Permanent role

This is an opportunity to step into a high‑impact leadership role in the Financial Markets Group (FMG) shaping the future of our iconic Australian institution.

About the team

FMG plays a critical role in implementing the Bank's domestic and foreign exchange operations, analysing market developments and supporting key policy objectives.

As Change Planning and Delivery Manager, you will partner with fellow leaders across FMG to shape, coordinate and support the Group's change portfolio. You'll collaborate to ensure initiatives are well planned, well-resourced and positioned for successful delivery through disciplined planning, analysis and change practices.

You will work closely with stakeholders across business and technology to align priorities, uplift delivery capability and ensure FMG's change initiatives are actionable, governed and aligned to strategic objectives. You'll also play a key role in the broader BSS leadership team, fostering collaboration and continuous improvement.

About the role


Lead portfolio‑level planning, resource forecasting and capacity management using contemporary portfolio management frameworks

Partner with FMG leaders to shape the change portfolio, guiding initiative sequencing and investment prioritisation.

Provide leadership to business analysts, testers and planning specialists

Coordinate release planning, change impacts and business readiness

Support business case development, procurement planning and product onboarding

Build strong relationships across technical and business stakeholders

Drive continuous improvement of delivery practices and planning frameworks



About you


Strong planning and prioritisation capability, with experience shaping sequencing, investment decisions and enabling fast adaptive decision‑making

Proven experience in resource forecasting, capacity planning and modern portfolio management aligning strategy with execution and value streams

Strong analytical thinking, communication and problem‑solving abilities

Excellent stakeholder engagement skills across business and technology domains

Leadership experience across business analysis, testing or similar functions

Experience leading change across complex environments, ideally financial services

Knowledge of Agile or hybrid delivery environments



Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

Based in Perth, Melbourne or Sydney
Lead delivery of enterprise-wide learning strategy and workforce capability
Partner with senior leaders to drive performance and transformation

Alinta Energy is one of Australia’s biggest energy retailers, generators and developers with over 1.1 million customers. Australia’s energy system is undergoing the biggest transformation in its history and we’re right there with it. We’re investing in and developing large-scale renewables and storage projects as part of our ongoing effort to provide our customers with reliable, affordable and sustainable energy.

We understand that flexibility matters when considering your next role. Let us know your requirements, and we’ll carefully consider how we might accommodate them.

Ready to make a real impact?

As Head of Learning & Development, lead the delivery of the enterprise learning and development strategy, ensuring strong alignment to organisational priorities while applying deep Learning & Development expertise to build current and future workforce capability and drive business performance.

Why you’ll love working here

At Alinta Energy, we’re taking real action to deliver reliable and sustainable energy for Australia — and that practical approach runs through everything we do. You’ll get clear direction, genuine flexibility, and the support you need to grow, all while working on projects that make a lasting difference. We’re big enough to create impact, but small enough that your ideas and effort will always be seen.

There are plenty of other great benefits of working for Alinta Energy, including flexible working, energy discounts, an Employee Assistance Program (EAP), a paid volunteer day each year, fully stocked kitchens, and lots more.

What you’ll do

Partner with senior leaders as a trusted advisor on workforce capability, future skills and upskilling and reskilling priorities.
Oversee the scoping and prioritisation of learning programs that build critical organisational capability and support business transformation.
Lead enterprise learning needs analysis in collaboration with People & Culture and business stakeholders to address capability gaps.
Build, coach, and lead a high-performing L&D team, fostering strong business partnering and a culture of continuous learning.
Oversee the effective implementation of learning initiatives, ensuring measurable impact, strong governance, and compliance with regulatory requirements.
Establish performance measures and use insights to continuously improve learning effectiveness and return on investment.[VN1] 

Your experience

A tertiary qualification in HR, Organisational Development, Education, Psychology, Business, or a related field, with postgraduate qualifications highly regarded.
Extensive experience (10+ years) in learning and development, ideally within complex industries such as Retail, Energy, or Construction, including leading enterprise-wide L&D strategies.
Demonstrated success leading and developing high-performing teams in fast-paced, evolving environments.
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with the ability to influence at senior levels.
Proven capability to measure learning effectiveness and ROI using data and insights.
A strategic, collaborative, and innovative mindset, with a strong commitment to continuous learning and organisational capability development

Please note this role requires full working rights within Australia.

This role is best suited to a strategic and collaborative leader who is passionate about building capability and enabling organisational performance through impactful learning.

Like what you’re seeing?

If you like the sound of this, and you’ve got experience in a similar role, we’d love to talk to you. Come and discover better together with Alinta Energy.

We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer – we commit to respond to every applicant.

If your application progresses, we will need to complete background and pre-employment checks, such as immigration checks, reference checks, criminal background checks, qualification checks, and additional checks for roles related to finance.

Our Diversity Commitment

Alinta Energy offers a truly diverse, safe and supportive work environment. As an equal opportunity employer, Alinta Energy encourages applications from any qualified person, including First Nations Australians, members of the LGBTQIA+ community, people of all ages and people living with a disability. We are also proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer and we are committed to advancing women’s careers.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Own and grow the Sparkling Beverage Maker (SBM) and CO2 & Accessories categories in Australia by translating consumer and customer insights into winning innovation, go-to-market and in-market execution.

Lead end-to-end category and product management across omnichannel, ensuring strong commercial outcomes (NSV, gross margin, A&M ROI) while delivering superior brand experiences and innovations.




Own and grow the Sparkling Beverage Maker and CO₂ & Accessories categories in Australia, with end‑to‑end responsibility for strategy, performance, profitability and execution across all channels.
Deliver the local category and innovation roadmap, grounded in consumer and shopper insights, competitor analysis and market trends, and aligned to global category direction.
Lead go‑to‑market strategy and launch execution, partnering cross‑functionally to ensure commercial readiness, strong in‑market execution and post‑launch performance.
Drive commercial outcomes, owning pricing, margin and channel architecture decisions, supported by robust analysis and clear consumer value logic.
Translate category strategy into customer outcomes through joint business planning, collaborative co‑op planning with Trade Marketing, retailer engagement and 'Perfect Store' execution across priority channels.
Own PMR within the IBP process, for portfolio review, identification of risks and opportunities, and aligned action plans.
Champion omni‑channel category excellence, including eCommerce content, assortment and promotional execution, ensuring consistent brand and category delivery.
Support experiential consumer events and brand activations, ensuring category relevance and contribution to a strong, engaging consumer experience.
Manage key category delivery and governance activities, including packaging and user manual artwork approvals, purchase order creation and receipting, and accurate management of marketing spend tracking to ensure compliance and on‑time delivery.

7+ years' experience in Product/Brand/Category Management within FMCG, consumer durables or a similar consumer-led environment; experience across both offline and eCommerce preferred.

Proven track record leading innovation/NPD from concept to launch, including GTM planning and cross-functional execution.

Strong commercial and analytical capability: pricing, margin management, business case development, performance reporting and optimisation.

Demonstrated customer/retailer partnership skills, including joint business planning, range reviews, presentations and negotiation support.

Experience operating within planning rhythms such as IBP/PMR, with strong attention to process, quality and deadlines.

Excellent communication and storytelling skills; able to build clear, persuasive decks for senior stakeholders and retailers.

Highly organised with strong project management skills; able to simplify ways of working and remove obstacles.

Comfortable collaborating with global teams and translating global direction into locally relevant plans.

Proficiency in Microsoft Office; familiarity with Workfront (or similar workflow tools) preferred.

CK Connect is partnering with a leading end-user organisation embarking on a major enterprise transformation programme. As part of a broader technology modernisation initiative, the organisation is implementing SAP S/4HANA to enhance core business systems and optimise operational processes.

We are seeking an experienced SAP S/4HANA Business Analyst to support the successful delivery of this transformation. This role will work closely with business stakeholders and technical teams to analyse current processes, gather requirements, and support the implementation of improved SAP solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Engage with business stakeholders to gather, analyse and document functional and system requirements for SAP S/4HANA initiatives.

Facilitate workshops to understand current business processes and define future-state solutions.

Translate business requirements into clear functional specifications for SAP delivery teams.

Analyse existing processes and identify opportunities for optimisation using SAP S/4HANA capabilities.

Support solution design, testing activities, and user acceptance testing.

Work closely with SAP functional consultants, technical teams and implementation partners.

Assist with data migration planning, reporting improvements and documentation.

Act as a key liaison between business stakeholders, project teams and IT.

Support change management and user adoption as part of the broader transformation programme.

Required Experience

Proven experience working as a Business Analyst on SAP S/4HANA projects.

Strong understanding of enterprise business processes such as Finance, Procurement, Operations or Supply Chain.

Experience facilitating business workshops and requirements gathering sessions.

Experience supporting SAP implementations or transformation programmes.

Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.

Ability to translate business needs into clear system requirements.

Desirable

Experience working within complex enterprise environments.

Exposure to end-to-end ERP implementation projects.

Familiarity with Agile or hybrid project delivery methodologies.

This role offers the opportunity to play a key role in a large-scale enterprise transformation programme involving modern SAP technologies.

With deep expertise in Managed Infrastructure Services, Application Modernization, and Industry-Specific Software Solutions, DXC modernizes, secures, and operates some of the world’s most complex technology estates. SAP Investigative Case Management (ICM) Lead

Responsible for the ICM solution including:
Incident Management (inc Public Interest Disclosure)
Case Management
Activities Management

Works with customer Security / Case Management experts to identify where SAP can assist during stabilisation and beyond to optimise outcomes and the knowledge and skills of the Case Management team

Senior AI Consultant - Sydney

💰 $170,000 + Super | Permanent Full-Time | Financial Services

Role Summary

We are seeking a Senior AI Consultant to operate across the full engagement lifecycle - from diagnosing where AI creates value to overseeing the build and delivery of solutions. You will bring structure to ambiguous problems and technical depth to delivery.

Key Responsibilities

Consulting & Value Creation

Diagnose operational and commercial challenges and translate them into AI investment cases.

Lead engagements end-to-end: scoping, delivery, and change management - with a clear line to operational KPIs.

Present recommendations to executives and stakeholders.

AI Due Diligence & Investment Support

Assess how AI affects competitive dynamics, cost structures, and growth trajectories.

Evaluate AI maturity, disruption risk, and post-acquisition value creation potential.

Technical Delivery

Rapidly prototype solutions to validate hypotheses before committing to full builds.

Stay current on emerging AI technologies and identify what genuinely moves the needle.

Qualifications, Skills & Experience

3-7 years in consulting, strategy, or technical problem-solving - ideally with exposure to PE-backed businesses.

Proven ability to run structured engagements: framing, stakeholder management, and on-time delivery.

Strong understanding of AI - able to separate signal from hype and provide practical guidance.

Hands-on experience with AI deployments and technical delivery.

Clear, confident communicator - comfortable in both technical deep-dives and executive briefings.

Exposure to frameworks and methodologies common in Big4 advisory or similar environments is highly regarded.

📩 Apply Now
Bring your AI expertise and strategic insight to a senior consulting role - helping shape AI-driven transformation in a dynamic, innovative environment.

We are currently seeking an Business / Systems Analyst to join a growing team and support the day-to-day operation of a core ERP platform within a fast paced environment.

This role will suit a hands-on problem solver who enjoys investigating system issues, working with data, and supporting business users. You'll play a key role in troubleshooting ERP-related issues, analysing data through SQL, and ensuring the smooth running of key business processes.

Key Responsibilities

Provide support for ERP-related issues raised by end users and the service desk.
Collaborate and partner with business stakeholders to gather technical platform requirements related to the migration.
Asses, review, and use of gathered data to support and participate in decision making and fact-based direction within the program.
Investigate and troubleshoot issues by analysing backend database tables using SQL.
Perform data extracts and analysis to assist with issue resolution and reporting.
Support the management of inventory and item/product master data, including pricing and cost information.
Work closely with internal stakeholders to triage, diagnose, and resolve system issues.
Assist with ongoing system improvements and operational support activities.
About You

Experience working in a Systems Analyst, Business Analyst, or Application Support role.
Strong SQL skills with the ability to query databases and investigate backend data issues.
Experience supporting ERP systems, ideally within a FMCG/retail or inventory-driven environment.
Exposure to inventory modules or product/item master data is highly desirable.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
Self-motivated with a positive attitude and willingness to learn.
If this position is of interest to you, click APPLY today or contact Sam Makdesi on smakdesi@synchropartners.com.au

Head of Technology and Innovation

Harwell Home Care is entering its next phase of digital evolution and we are looking for a hands-on technology leader to build it with us.

This is not a traditional Head of IT role.

We are seeking a strong senior engineer who still loves building, shipping, and solving complex problems and who is ready to broaden into enterprise technology leadership.

You will lead our technology capability across engineering and IT operations, while remaining actively involved in architecture, software development, and AI-enabled innovation.

About Harwell

Harwell is a rapidly growing healthcare organisation delivering high-quality community-based care. As we scale, technology is central to how we operate, from workforce optimisation and automation to AI-driven systems and digital client experiences.

We build internally and externally. We move fast. We solve real operational problems with modern technology.

The Role

As Head of Technology & Innovation, you will:

Architect scalable backend systems and integrations
Drive practical AI implementation across our applications and internal workflows
Lead and mentor developers as the engineering function grows
Provide strategic oversight of IT operations, cybersecurity posture, and infrastructure governance
Oversee IT partners and operational support resources to ensure reliability and compliance
Develop and execute a 2–3 year technology roadmap
Work directly with executive leadership and the Board on technology strategy

This role is expected to be 50–70% hands-on engineering and architecture, with the remainder focused on strategy, governance, and oversight.

If you no longer want to code, this is not the role for you.

What You’ll Be Building With

React / Next.js
React Native / Expo
TypeScript / Node
PostgreSQL / Supabase
API-driven integrations
Boomi AI tooling and intelligent workflows

What We’re Looking For

You are likely:

A Principal Engineer, Lead Engineer, or Technical Lead
Comfortable owning architecture end-to-end
Experienced leading or mentoring developers
Confident overseeing IT operations or security governance at a strategic level
Actively using AI tools in your development workflow
Commercially minded - you build solutions that solve real business problems
Interested in stepping toward broader technology leadership

Experience with enterprise integrations, cybersecurity governance, or Microsoft 365 security is highly regarded.

Why This Role Is Different

This is an opportunity to:

Shape the technology function of a growing healthcare organisation
Lead both engineering innovation and enterprise technology governance
Build and scale a modern tech team
Influence board-level technology strategy
Grow toward a future CIO pathway as the organisation scales

You will not manage from a distance.

You will build, lead, and scale

Please submit your application as soon as possible, as we reserve the right to close this advertisement at any time.


Cochlear's mission is to help people hear and be heard. As the global leader in implantable hearing solutions, Cochlear is dedicated to helping people with moderate to profound hearing loss experience a world full of sound. We aim to transform the way people understand and treat hearing loss and innovate to connect people with future technologies. Our employees tell us that the number one reason they enjoy working for Cochlear is the opportunity to make a difference to people's lives. Learn and grow with us as we tackle the most complex challenges in helping more people to experience a lifetime of hearing.

We are seeking an experienced Program Financial Manager to play a hands‑on, delivery‑focused role within our major SAP transformation program. This 12 month fixed‑term position is responsible for the day‑to‑day and forward‑looking financial management of the program, ensuring the right financial insights, controls, and governance are in place to support high‑quality executive and Steering Committee decision‑making.

Embedded within the Program Leadership team and PMO, this role suits someone who thrives in large‑scale transformation, enjoys partnering with delivery leaders, and is comfortable operating with urgency, precision, and accountability.


What You'll Be Doing
Program Financial Control
Own the integrated financial position for the entire SAP program (actuals, forecasts, outlook).
Ensure spending remains within the approved funding envelope.
Identify financial risks, pressures, and variances early.
Maintain clear audit trails and governance consistent with organisational standards.
Forecasting, Reporting & Executive Support
Produce monthly financial reporting for Program Leadership and the Steering Committee.
Deliver accurate variance analysis and forward‑looking commentary.
Support rolling forecast cycles with disciplined assumptions aligned to delivery plans.
Provide scenario and sensitivity analysis to support deployment, phasing, and scope decisions.
Change Request & Contingency Governance
Lead financial assessments of program change requests.
Evaluate cost, funding source, contingency impact, and forecasting implications.
Track and report contingency usage, approvals, and remaining balances.
Partner with PMO and Workstream Leads to ensure financial impacts are understood before approvals.
Vendor & Commercial Financial Management
Manage financial performance of major delivery partners (SAP, SI partners, specialist vendors).
Track burn rates, validate invoices and accruals, and monitor contracted vs actual spend.
Support commercial discussions with fact‑based financial analysis.
Oversee monthly accruals processes and ensure accuracy of vendor financials.
Stakeholder Engagement
Act as a trusted financial advisor to the Program Director, PMO Lead, and Workstream Leads.
Translate complex data into clear, decision‑ready insights.
Provide direction to any supporting finance analysts (without formal people management responsibility).
What You Bring
Essential
10+ years' experience in program or transformation financial management, ideally within large ERP/SAP programs.
Strong track record managing complex technology transformation budgets.
Deep understanding of major IT program cost drivers (SIs, licensing, environments, migration, testing, deployment).
Demonstrated experience supporting Steering Committees and executive forums.
Advanced financial modelling, forecasting, and variance analysis capability.
Desirable
Experience in global or phased SAP deployments.
Exposure to highly governed or regulated environments.
Prior experience in FTC/contract roles within transformation programs.
Qualifications
Degree in Finance, Accounting, Commerce, or related field.
CPA, CA, or CIMA qualification (preferred).
Why Join Us?
This is a rare opportunity to influence one of the organisation's most strategically important programs. You'll work closely with senior leaders, shape financial decision‑making, and play a critical role in driving effective delivery of a large‑scale SAP transformation.

If you're a proactive, delivery‑focused finance professional who enjoys partnering with program teams and influencing outcomes-we'd love to hear from you.


How we recognise your contribution

At Cochlear we value and welcome the unique contributions, perspectives, experiences, and backgrounds of our employees and aim to build a culture that celebrates and leverages these differences, creating a sense of belonging and enabling our people to realise their full potential. Through our internal programs and employee benefits, we aim to create an environment where our people will feel value and supported. Whether your focus is on continuous learning, professional development or simply finding an environment which enables you to thrive whilst balancing family or personal life commitments, then we have several programs in place to support you.

For more information about Life at Cochlear, visit www.cochlearcareers.com

Job Title: SAP Analytics Consultant - Support (Anywhere in Australia)
Ad Text:
DyFlex Solutions is an established SAP Platinum Partner with offices across Australia and New Zealand. Recognised as a leader in SAP expertise, we deliver fit‑for‑purpose solutions ranging from greenfield and brownfield implementations to change management, Aftercare services, and ongoing support.
In 2025, we were proud to be awarded:

✓ Best Performing Partner in Australia for S/4HANA Public Cloud
✓ Best Performing Partner in Australia for Speed of Delivery
✓ Best Performing Partner in Mid-Market
✓ Highest Selling Partner in Australia

About the Role

We are seeking an SAP Analytics Consultant to join our national Aftercare team, supporting a diverse portfolio of customers across multiple industries. This role focuses on day‑to‑day operational support and continuous improvement across the SAP Analytics landscape, including BW, BW/4HANA, BO, SAC, Datasphere, and Embedded Analytics.

You will work closely with business and functional teams to maintain reporting reliability, ensure data accuracy, deliver enhancements, and support integrations across SAP and non‑SAP environments. This role is ideal for a consultant who thrives in a customer‑focused support environment and enjoys solving real‑world reporting and analytics challenges.

This position can be performed remotely from anywhere along the East Coast of Australia, with occasional travel for key customer engagements.

Key Responsibilities
Provide day‑to‑day support for BAU activities, including incident resolution, service requests, improvement requests, and system monitoring.
Maintain and enhance SAP Analytics systems to ensure data quality, reporting accuracy, and overall system reliability.
Collaborate with business users and functional teams to understand reporting requirements and deliver high‑quality solutions.
Support upgrades, testing cycles, transports, and release activities as required.
Work across integrations involving SAP source systems and non‑SAP data sources.
Maintain accurate documentation of system configuration, support procedures, and troubleshooting steps.
Contribute to a high‑performing Aftercare function through proactive problem‑solving and customer engagement.
Qualifications & Experience
Relevant tertiary qualifications in a technical or commercial field—finance or accounting highly regarded.
Hands‑on experience with SAP Analytics tools: BW, BW/4HANA, BO, SAC, Datasphere, and Embedded Analytics.
Functional understanding of SAP S/4HANA environments (on‑premise, private cloud, and public cloud).
Strong grasp of system scope, integration landscapes, software configuration, and analytics architecture.
Proven ability to work confidently with customers, team members, and stakeholders at all levels.
Strong time‑management capabilities and ability to work effectively within a team‑based support environment.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Full‑time permanent candidates only; we are not seeking contractors.

Ad Text:
HACC Transformation Lead

6 Month Full-Time Contract (Potential Extension to FY27)

Sydney CBD | Hybrid Working

Australia’s in-home aged care system is undergoing one of the most significant reforms in decades.

Following the Royal Commission into Aged Care, the sector is transitioning to a new model designed to help older Australians live independently in their homes for longer. The new Support at Home program, introduced in late 2025, replaces Home Care Packages and Short-Term Restorative Care, with further transition of community support programs expected by 2027.

For providers, this means large-scale transformation across service models, funding structures, workforce capability and operational delivery.

And that’s where this role comes in.

We’re looking for an HACC Transformation Lead to help drive the engine room of this transformation.




The Opportunity

Working within a high-impact transformation office, you will drive and coordinate transformation initiatives across Home and Community Care, ensuring projects deliver measurable outcomes for the organisation, our workforce and ultimately the communities we serve.

You’ll play a pivotal role in identifying opportunities, designing and tracking initiatives, measuring progress and ensuring the transformation delivers real value.

This is an initial 6-month full-time contract, with the potential to extend through to the end of FY27 aligned with broader sector reforms.




What You’ll Be Responsible For

As the HACC Transformation Lead, you will:

Develop and periodically refresh Uniting Home Care Transformation project pipeline
Drive the initiative design work, collaborating with project Sponsors, the Home Care Operations and Uniting SMEs 
Develop and execute detailed initiative plans aligned to major transformation milestones
Develop recommendations for sequencing and prioritisation transformation initiatives across multiple workstreams
Develop reporting tools to track program performance and identify underperforming initiatives
Analyse program outcomes and generate insights that inform decision-making
Validate financial benefits and track operational KPIs of the program
Engage and influence stakeholders across the organisation, including senior leadership and community care partners
Monitor workstream risks and implement mitigation strategies
Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and evolving industry standards
Translate complex data and trends into actionable insights and service improvement opportunities




What You Bring

Bachelor’s degree in business, Healthcare Management, or similar
2–3+ years’ experience in analytical, transformation, or program environments
Strong Excel capability and confidence working with large datasets
Demonstrated ability to support or lead large-scale organisational change
Solid project management skills with the ability to manage competing priorities
A highly analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into insights
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Experience or familiarity with Home and Community Care / Aged Care regulatory environments is desirable but not necessary




Why This Role Matters

The future of aged care in Australia is shifting rapidly. With new funding models, service structures and regulatory expectations being introduced across the sector, organisations must evolve quickly to continue delivering high-quality community care.

This role sits at the heart of that transformation.

You’ll help shape how services are delivered, how teams operate and how we support older Australians to remain connected to their communities and live with dignity at home.




If you’re someone who enjoys working in the engine room of transformation, thrives on data, insights and change, and wants to contribute to meaningful reform in community care, we’d love to hear from you.




About Uniting:

When you’re part of Uniting NSW.ACT, you’re part of a diverse, purpose-led team of people who are really making a difference to the world around them.

We contribute to the work and mission of the Uniting Church in NSW and the ACT through social justice advocacy, community services and spiritual care. We provide care and support for people through all ages and stages of life, with a focus on people experiencing disadvantage and vulnerability. Our purpose is to inspire people, enliven communities and confront injustice.

Uniting leaders and employees work together to create a culture that is safe, inclusive and person-centred. We bring this to life every day through our values: compassionate, respectful, imaginative and bold.

Uniting acknowledges the continuing sovereignty and rich cultural diversity of Australia’s First Peoples. We pay our respects to all Elders – past, present, and emerging – and to all First Peoples on whose lands we live and work.

Uniting is a Child Safe Organisation committed to promoting the safety, wellbeing and inclusion of children and young people.

About Us 

Study Hubs Australia (SHA) is a not-for-profit organisation that works alongside study hubs, universities, and governments to create local pathways to tertiary education through the community-led study sub model. We believe every Australian should be able to access and succeed in tertiary education within their community. Our focus is on embedding study hubs as an enduring and effective part of Australia’s tertiary education system, so access to tertiary education is not limited by where people live.  

Study Hubs Australia builds the shared evidence, policy and practice that communities, tertiary education providers, and governments need to support effective Study Hub operations and studying in place. 

The Role 

The Head of Evaluation and Impact leads Study Hubs Australia’s evaluation and impact strategy and practice, with a focus on community-led approaches and building evaluation capability across Australian study hubs. The role will also lead the evaluation of community-led widening participation activities across 20 Regional University Study Hubs.  

This role sits at the intersection of impact measurement, community development, and system advocacy. It requires someone who can combine methodological rigor with accessible, practical approaches; communicate findings with clarity and influence; and represent the Study Hub model confidently with governments, universities, funders, and communities. 

What We’re Looking For 

We’re looking for a strategic and hands-on evaluation leader who can turn evidence into impact and help strengthen equitable access to tertiary education across Australia. In this role you will bring: 

A passion for expanding equitable access to tertiary education, with the ability to embed evaluation thinking across organisations and work confidently in fast-moving, collaborative environments. 

A postgraduate qualification in evaluation, social science, education, public policy, health sciences or a related field, with strong research and evaluation expertise. 

Demonstrated experience designing and leading program evaluations, including program logic, theory of change, and mixed-methods approaches to measure impact and track outcomes. 

The ability to turn data and evidence into clear insights, producing compelling reports and presentations that inform decisions across communities, universities and government. 

Experience working in complex stakeholder environments, ideally across higher education, regional communities and/or not-for-profit sectors. 

Strong leadership and project management capability, including managing staff, coordinating multiple priorities, and delivering projects to clear timelines.

Xenith is a leading consulting firm supporting the Resources, Energy and Infrastructure sectors across the full asset lifecycle. Our Advisory team works at the intersection of technical, financial and strategic decision-making — supporting corporates, investors and financiers on critical transactions and investments.

We are seeking a Principal Analyst – Advisory to strengthen our senior analytical capability and provide trusted, defensible advice to clients across the resources sector.

This is a senior, hands-on role for a highly analytical professional who is comfortable owning complex financial and technical analysis, engaging directly with clients, and contributing to high-value investment and transaction decisions.

The Opportunity

Based in Sydney/Brisbane or Perth, this role is suited to an experienced mining, investment or financial advisory professional operating at Principal / Senior Consultant level.

You will work across a diverse portfolio of projects supporting mining companies, financial institutions, private equity, legal and accounting firms.

You will:

• Lead and deliver high-quality analytical work across advisory engagements

• Act as a trusted advisor to clients at technical, commercial and executive level

• Take ownership of complex models, assumptions and conclusions

• Provide senior input into transactions, valuations and investment decisions

• Mentor junior analysts and lift analytical standards across the team

This is not a coordination or reporting-only role. You will remain close to the analysis, the detail and the decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Analytical Leadership & Advisory Delivery

• Lead and review complex financial, technical and commercial analysis

• Develop and interrogate financial models for mining and resource assets

• Apply strong judgement to assumptions, sensitivities and scenarios

• Ensure analytical outputs are robust, defensible and decision-ready




Transaction & Investment Support

• Support M&A transactions and investment decisions

• Deliver technical due diligence and expert assessments

• Contribute to mineral asset valuations and investment evaluations

• Support lenders, investors and boards with clear, evidence-based advice




Client Advisory & Communication

• Act as a client-facing advisor, presenting findings clearly and confidently

• Translate complex technical and financial analysis into clear insights

• Prepare high-quality reports, expert opinions and advisory papers

• Support expert witness work and independent advisory engagements


Mentoring & Capability Building

• Mentor analysts and consultants across the Advisory team

• Lift analytical rigour, modelling standards and commercial thinking

• Contribute to internal methodologies, templates and best practice




About You

You are an analytical, commercially astute professional who thrives in complex problem-solving environments.

You have experience as a professional analyst in or serving the mining and resources industry, preferably underpinned by demonstrable experience in a technical discipline.

You are comfortable standing behind your work and explaining it to sophisticated stakeholders.

You thrive in a client serving, demanding and dynamic consulting environment.


Skills, Qualifications & Experience

• Degree in Mining Engineering, Finance, Economics, Geology or related discipline

• Typically 8–15+ years’ experience in mining, investment, financial or technical advisory

• Exceptional analytical skills with strong attention to detail

• Advanced financial modelling and analytical capability

• Experience across mining projects, transactions or resource investments

• Strong written and verbal communication skills

• Comfortable working in a client-facing consulting environment

• Commercially aware, pragmatic and outcome-focused




Why Xenith

• High-trust, technically strong consulting culture

• Exposure to complex, high-impact advisory work

• Flexible working arrangements

• Competitive remuneration aligned to seniority and accountability

Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills.

Strong vendor management experience.
Highly organised, proactive, and comfortable working to tight deadlines.

Job Description


About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

What you’ll do

Lead and grow a high-performing team of Machine Learning Engineers focused on automating content review and moderation across Canva’s Content Library.

Set and execute the technical vision and strategy for ML-driven safety, quality, and policy enforcement across text, image, video, audio and template content.

Design and scale robust ML systems — including experimentation frameworks, evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and feedback loops to continuously improve model performance and close the gap between manual review and automation.

Partner cross-functionally with Content, Product, Review Ops, and Review Engineering to deliver impactful, user-facing solutions and scalable human-in-the-loop systems.

Drive large, end-to-end initiatives from problem framing and roadmap definition through to delivery, experimentation, rollout, and impact measurement.

Uplift engineering excellence through strong technical direction, pragmatic architecture decisions, and a healthy code review and knowledge-sharing culture.

Coach and mentor engineers to navigate complex technical challenges and do the best work of their lives.

Champion operational excellence, responsible AI, and safety-by-design, ensuring our systems serve all users, including students and educators, with quality, integrity, and trust.

Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging ML best practices, continuously evolving the domain to meet Canva’s scale and ambition.

You're probably a match if

You’re passionate about coaching and developing engineers, creating a culture of growth and support.

You’re experienced in designing systems at scale, with a focus on reliability, performance, and extensibility.

You collaborate cross-functionally and thrive in partnerships with product, ML, and content stakeholders.

You think strategically and deliver pragmatically, balancing long-term vision with immediate needs.

You value diversity, empathy, and inclusion, and lead in a way that fosters belonging and trust.

You have experience leading an engineering team in a Product or SaaS environment, ideally within ML or applied AI domains.

Prior experience in text, image, video, and audio understanding is highly desirable.

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

Ringfenced from client delivery and support — pure internal build work

You’ll be fully embedded in the internal integration team, working closely with leaders in reconciliation and product. No client delivery. No support queue. Just meaningful, analytical build work.




What You’ll Be Doing

Building reconciliation processes (Ledger Matching and bank‑to‑book)

Mapping, cleaning and structuring financial datasets

Designing transformation workflows and exception logic

Documenting assumptions, control logic and data journeys

Ensuring processes are robust, testable and edge‑case ready

This is analytical and process‑oriented — not a development role — but very hands‑on with data.




What You Bring

Core skills:

Strong SQL (joins, filtering, aggregation, transformation)

Ad Text:
Freelancer.com, a thirteen-time Webby award-winning global technology company, operates the world’s largest freelancing and crowdsourcing marketplace, connecting over 85 million users who have posted more than 25 million projects. Our clients range from individual consumers to organisations like NASA, spanning diverse sectors including website development, marketing, astrophysics, aerospace engineering, and manufacturing.

Our portfolio also includes Escrow.com, the leading global online escrow company, securing over US$8 billion in transactions, and Loadshift, Australia’s largest heavy haulage freight marketplace.

Listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX: FLN), Freelancer.com is seeking an ambitious, proactive, and commercially-astute Chief Financial Officer. This is a high-impact executive leadership role for a finance professional ready to drive the financial strategy of a dynamic, fast-paced global group.

Role Overview

Reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer, the CFO will be a key member of the executive leadership team. You will lead the global finance function, oversee statutory and regulatory compliance, and act as a strategic partner to the CEO and Board. You will thrive in an entrepreneurial setting, driving capital allocation strategy, managing investor relations, and ensuring the financial health of a multi-product global marketplace.

Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership: Partner with the CEO and Board to define and execute the Group’s financial strategy, capital management, and long-term growth objectives.
Financial Reporting & Planning: Oversee the preparation and presentation of comprehensive financial reports, budgets, and rolling forecasts for all global entities.
Investor Relations: Lead the Group’s investor relations strategy, managing communications with shareholders, conducting analyst briefings, and representing the company at investor conferences.
ASX Compliance: Manage all ASX listing rule requirements, including half-year and annual reporting, continuous disclosure obligations, and regulatory filings.
Operational Finance: Enhance and automate global financial systems and processes to drive efficiency across multiple jurisdictions.
Strategic Analysis: Lead business modelling, risk assessment, and financial due diligence for M&A, new product launches, and strategic initiatives.
Audit & Tax: Coordinate global external audits and manage tax compliance and planning across various international jurisdictions.
Commercial Advisory: Provide high-level commercial insights on legal agreements, complex contracts, and strategic partnerships.
Capital Allocation: Drive cost-efficiency and performance metrics across the Group’s portfolio (Freelancer, Escrow, and Loadshift) to maximise shareholder value.
Requirements
Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree in Finance or Accounting. CA/CPA is required.
Experience: 15+ years of progressive finance experience, with a significant portion in senior leadership (CFO, VP Finance, or Head of Finance) within an ASX-listed environment.
Public Markets: Proven track record in public market reporting, corporate governance, and high-level investor relations.
International Scope: Extensive experience in international transactions, multi-currency consolidations, and global tax structures.
Domain Expertise: Deep interest or experience in technology-driven businesses, online marketplaces, or fintech.
Technical Excellence: Exceptional financial modelling capabilities and the ability to translate complex data into actionable strategic insights.
Communication: A sophisticated communicator able to engage effectively with the Board, institutional investors, and global teams.
Legal Acumen: Proven ability to navigate, review, and interpret complex legal and regulatory contracts.
Mindset: An energetic, proactive, and entrepreneurial attitude with meticulous attention to detail and a "hands-on" approach to problem-solving.
Why Freelancer?

At Freelancer, our team drives global impact every day. We provide opportunities for millions worldwide, enabling them to earn a living, build businesses, and accomplish remarkable things. As a meritocratic company, we celebrate initiative, innovation, and outcomes. Join our team to help shape the future of work and leave a lasting impact on millions around the globe.

At ING, we’re on a mission to become Australia’s most loved mobile bank — and that starts with delivering simple, seamless and meaningful experiences for our customers. If you’re energised by turning insight into action, lifting capability across an organisation, and shaping the future of customer experience, this is your moment.

At ING, we’re all about making life simpler and more rewarding - for the people who bank with us, the team members who work with us, and the communities we’re proud to support. Joining ING means stepping into an environment where your individuality isn’t just welcomed - it’s celebrated. We’ve built a culture that’s fun, inclusive, and supportive, giving you the freedom to be yourself, so you can do your thing.

Job Title: Principal Consultant - Azure Data Engineer. Brisbane
Ad Text:
Azure Data Engineer - Principal Consultant. Brisbane

About Us

At Agile Insights, we are passionate about empowering organisations to transform through the power of Data & AI. We work with global brands and leading organisations to deliver innovative solutions that solve real business challenges. We have a fantastic, long-standing relationship with Microsoft. We are extremely proud to be a Microsoft Solutions Specialist for four competencies including: Data Analytics and AI, Azure and Digital & App Innovation.

Your Role

We are seeking an experienced and dynamic Azure Data Engineer Principal Consultant to lead data engineering engagements, design scalable data solutions, and help clients unlock the full potential of their data. You will act as a trusted advisor, providing technical leadership and strategic guidance while working on cutting-edge Microsoft Azure technologies.

Key Responsibilities

Technical Leadership

Design and implement scalable, secure, and high-performing data pipelines and data platforms on Microsoft Azure.

Lead solution architecture discussions for Azure-based data engineering solutions, including data lakes, data warehouses, and data integration pipelines.

Advise clients on best practices, governance frameworks, and modern data architecture strategies.

Drive innovation by leveraging Microsoft services such as Microsoft Fabric, Azure Synapse, Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Data Lake, and Azure Purview.

Technical Management & Delivery

Oversee the end-to-end delivery of data engineering projects, ensuring quality, scope, and timelines are met.

Manage project teams, ensuring efficient task delegation and mentoring team members in Azure tools and techniques.

Collaborate with stakeholders to define technical requirements and deliver solutions aligned with business goals.

Client Engagement

Act as a trusted advisor to clients, providing insights and recommendations for data strategy and architecture.

Conduct workshops, technical demonstrations, and proof-of-concept (POC) projects to showcase Azure's capabilities.

Foster long-term client relationships and identify opportunities for future engagements.

Innovation & Knowledge Sharing

Stay updated on emerging trends in Azure Data and AI technologies and share knowledge with internal teams.

Develop reusable frameworks, accelerators, and best practices to enhance project delivery efficiency.

Contribute to thought leadership initiatives, including whitepapers, blogs, and conference presentations.

Collaborate with Microsoft and other partners to secure funding and co-develop innovative solutions. 

Practice Growth:

Support pre-sales efforts by crafting proposals, developing demos, and estimating project delivery costs. 

Contribute to Agile Insights' thought leadership by participating in industry events, panels, and conferences. 

Build and nurture a network of client relationships, identifying opportunities to expand Agile Insights’ presence in the market. 

Required Skills & Qualifications

Experience:

8+ years of experience in data engineering, with at least 5 years working with Microsoft Azure technologies.

Proven experience in delivering large-scale data solutions for enterprise clients.

Technical Expertise:

Hands-on experience with Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Data Lake, and Azure SQL Database.

Strong proficiency in data modelling, ETL/ELT processes, and designing scalable data pipelines.

Proficiency in Python, PySpark, SQL, or similar programming languages.

Knowledge of cloud security, compliance, and governance frameworks.

Knowledge on Microsoft Fabric is highly desirable.

Hands-on experience on CI/CD deployments using Azure DevOps.

Familiarity with BI tools like Power BI is a plus.

Certifications (preferred):

Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate

Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert

Microsoft Certified: Fabric Analytics Engineer Associate

Other relevant Azure certifications

Soft Skills:

Strong communication and presentation skills to engage with technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Leadership abilities, including mentoring junior engineers and managing project teams.

Problem-solving mindset with a focus on innovation and client success.

What’s In It for You?

At Agile Insights, we believe in nurturing talent and creating a supportive, flexible, and rewarding workplace. Here’s what you can look forward to:

Wellbeing: Access to hybrid work arrangements, an Employee Assistance Program, and an individualised gym and wellbeing program.

Flexibility: Work from anywhere for up to 6 weeks per year.

Professional Development: Additional paid leave for certifications and exams, with Agile-sponsored training programs for Microsoft and Databricks certifications.

Team Engagement: Enjoy regular team meetups, including state-based gatherings and an annual all-company event.

Equipment & Hardware: Receive an allowance for your home office setup and a 2-year equipment incentive.

Salary Packaging: Access novated leasing services for tax-effective car purchases.

Learning Resources: Expand your skills with access to our digital library and learning platforms. Opportunity to work with some of the best developers in the business including one on one mentoring with senior consultants.

Why Agile Insights?

Work with some of the largest clients and global brands across diverse industries.

Be part of a fast-growing, award-winning team passionate about transforming organisations with Data & AI.

Thrive in a friendly start-up culture that values innovation, collaboration, and work-life balance.

Ready to Lead with Us?

If you’re passionate about analytics, consulting, and community engagement, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now to join Agile Insights and take your career to the next level!

No Agencies - No overseas applications - Must be in Australia and have full work rights

Ad Text:
At ASIC, you can be the change that ensures a fair, strong and efficient financial system for the benefit of all Australians. Contribute to delivering on ASIC's purpose, vision, and strategic priorities to help maintain the integrity of the financial system and protect consumers from harm.

 

ASIC's Business Analysis team is currently seeking experienced Business Analysts to join their high-performing team and contribute to a range of strategic Cyber Security, Infrastructure and Data Governance projects.

24 month fixed term position
Salary from $152,074 to $158,666 (depending on experience) + 15.4 % superannuation

The team 

The Digital, Data and Technology Portfolio Office provides a wide range of project and support services to ASIC. We are undertaking major technology projects that will transform our business and continue our significant technology transformation in several areas such as visualisation, mobility and cloud.

 

The role

More specifically, you will:

Support UAT preparation and system implementation activities, including the creation of user manuals and training materials as required.
Demonstrate strong self-management and accountability in delivering outcomes aligned with ASIC's strategic priorities and team objectives.

 

About you

The ideal candidate this role will be an experienced, proactive and detail-oriented Business Analyst professional who possess strong analytical and communication skills. They will excel at translating complex business needs into clear, actionable requirements and thrive working within a collaborative environment with both business and IT stakeholders.

 

In addition, you will have:

Tertiary qualifications in Information Technology or a related discipline.
Strong understanding of Systems Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and Agile delivery methodologies.
Experience working in large, complex organisations with exposure to cyber security initiatives.
Solid knowledge of security frameworks, concepts and controls.
Demonstrated experience in infrastructure project delivery.
Proven capability in application onboarding, access provisioning and lifecycle management.
Familiarity with data governance platforms and practices.
Experience implementing and managing Privileged Access Management (PAM) solutions.
Understanding of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) technologies and enterprise integration.
Awareness of AI-powered productivity tools and their application in business environments.
Working knowledge of Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) frameworks.

 

Click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications will close at 11:59pm AEDT, Tuesday 17 March 2026.

Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms.

ASIC's Business Analysis team is currently seeking experienced Business Analysts to join their high-performing team and contribute to a range of strategic Cyber Security, Infrastructure and Data Governance projects.

 

Sydney or Melbourne locations available
24 month fixed term position
Salary from $152,074 to $158,666 (depending on experience) + 15.4 % superannuation
Applications will close at 11:59pm AEDT, Tuesday 17 March 2026 

 

The team 

The Digital, Data and Technology Portfolio Office provides a wide range of project and support services to ASIC. We are undertaking major technology projects that will transform our business and continue our significant technology transformation in several areas such as visualisation, mobility and cloud.

 

The role

As a Cyber Security Business Analyst, you will be responsible for leading key analytical activities to ensure successful project delivery. Responsibilities include high-level and detailed requirements elicitation and management, enterprise analysis, solution assessment, validation and analysis of both functional and non-functional requirements.

 

More specifically, you will: 

Collaborate with business SMEs, Cyber Security team and IT teams to analyse requirements and uncover underlying needs through stakeholder workshops and discussions.
Document functional and non-functional requirements in clear, accessible formats such as business requirements documents or user stories tailored to diverse stakeholders including Cyber Security, Business, Executives, Designers, Developers and Testers.
Rapidly develop an understanding of the technology landscape, business structure and strategic priorities to assess their impact on team deliverables.
Support UAT preparation and system implementation activities, including the creation of user manuals and training materials as required.
Demonstrate strong self-management and accountability in delivering outcomes aligned with ASIC's strategic priorities and team objectives.

 

About you

The ideal candidate this role will be an experienced, proactive and detail-oriented Business Analyst professional who possess strong analytical and communication skills. They will excel at translating complex business needs into clear, actionable requirements and thrive working within a collaborative environment with both business and IT stakeholders.

 

In addition, you will have:

Tertiary qualifications in Information Technology or a related discipline.
Strong understanding of Systems Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and Agile delivery methodologies.
Experience working in large, complex organisations with exposure to cyber security initiatives.
Solid knowledge of security frameworks, concepts and controls.
Demonstrated experience in infrastructure project delivery.
Proven capability in application onboarding, access provisioning and lifecycle management.
Familiarity with data governance platforms and practices.
Experience implementing and managing Privileged Access Management (PAM) solutions.
Understanding of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) technologies and enterprise integration.
Awareness of AI-powered productivity tools and their application in business environments.
Working knowledge of Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) frameworks.

 

Click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications will close at 11:59pm AEDT, Tuesday 17 March 2026.

 

About ASIC

ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms. 

ASIC is an equal opportunity employer seeking people who want to make a difference. ASIC is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Applications from people of all ages, cultural backgrounds, abilities, LGBTIQ+ identity, and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander decent are encouraged to apply.

 

We offer a range of employee benefits including:

Attractive superannuation
Additional leave entitlements
50/50 hybrid work-from home model
Flexible work arrangements
Assistance for study and professional development

 

Click here to view ASIC's salary and benefits guide.

To read more about ASIC, you can visit our website or review our Corporate Plan.

About the role – Full-time, 12-month fixed term contract

Be part of our transformation journey and join us as Senior Delivery Lead and help shape the digital banking experience that powers our members every day.

Lead the delivery of large, complex strategic initiatives that drive meaningful transformation and value across the organisation. Partner closely with Product Owners and senior stakeholders to turn strategy into outcomes, while enabling high-performing, self-organising teams to deliver with quality, pace and impact. This is an opportunity to shape how change is delivered across a major transformation portfolio while mentoring and uplifting delivery capability.

Key responsibilities include:  

·        Leading the delivery of large and complex strategic initiatives within a program/value stream, ensuring prioritised backlogs and technical projects are delivered with quality, pace and measurable business value.

·        Partnering closely with the Product Owner and business stakeholders to translate strategy into delivery outcomes, ensuring initiatives drive value creation, business adoption and successful value realisation.

·        Creating high-performing, self-organising delivery teams by mentoring delivery resources, removing blockers, and fostering a culture of collaboration, continuous improvement and delivery excellence.

·        Driving end-to-end project execution including planning, sprint/iteration management, dependency coordination, risk and issue management, and deployment activities across Agile, Waterfall or hybrid environments.

·        Building strong executive and stakeholder relationships, maintaining transparent communication with business owners, program leadership and sponsors on progress, risks, financials and outcomes.

·        Ensuring disciplined governance and financial oversight, managing budgets, forecasts, quality controls and cross-program dependencies to optimise delivery outcomes across the broader change portfolio.

About you 

You will succeed in this role if you have:

·        Extensive project delivery experience with a proven track record managing large, complex strategic initiatives within enterprise environments.

·        Strong expertise across Agile, Waterfall or hybrid delivery methodologies, with the ability to adapt approaches to maximise delivery outcomes.

·        Exceptional stakeholder engagement and influencing skills, with the confidence to work closely with Product Owners, executives and cross-functional teams.

·        Ability to lead and mentor delivery teams, fostering high-performing, self-organising teams and promoting a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement.

·        Strong problem-solving and analytical mindset, comfortable navigating complexity, managing competing priorities and removing delivery blockers.

·        Sound financial and governance capability, including managing budgets, forecasts, risks, issues and dependencies across multiple initiatives.

·        Experience delivering change within financial services or similarly regulated environments, with an understanding of business, technology and operational interdependencies.

·        A proactive, delivery-focused mindset, committed to driving quality outcomes, business adoption and measurable value realisation.

Location

Whilst our primary office locations are in Adelaide and Brisbane, we are pleased to be able to consider applications from employees within Australia for this role. We also encourage you to put thought into how the work may be undertaken given your location and what support you may require.

Why you’ll love working with us: 

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Enhance your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

 A bit about People First Bank:  

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contract centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

Apply online now:

If this sounds like you, this could be the next step in your career. Click 'Apply' now and submit your application, including a cover letter and current resume by COB Tuesday, 24th March 2026.

We are committed to diversity and inclusion and support candidate requests for adjustments to enable everyone to equitably participate in our selection process.

About The Role
Financial Specialist required for a fixed term contract for an initial 18 month project assisting the finance area to implement Dynamics 365. This role will be instrumental in providing hands on guidance from design through to go live and go live support. You will be the SME withing this space advising between finance and the business.

Responsibilities will include:
• Lead finance input into the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (F&O) implementation, partnering with the ERP Lead and external implementation partners.
• Provide financial and accounting expertise to ensure system design meets accounting standards, reporting requirements and project operations needs.
• Work with finance, project and operational teams to define and document future state workflows for F&O and Project Operations, driving standardisation and best practice outcomes.
• Design and embed robust internal controls and approval workflows, including segregation of duties, approval hierarchies and automated controls.
• Ensure the system supports statutory, management and tax reporting requirements, working with Financial Assurance to validate governance, audit trails and controls.
• Lead financial data cleansing and migration activities, ensuring accuracy, reconciliation and data integrity across legacy and new systems.
• Coordinate finance testing activities, including UAT, developing test scenarios and supporting issue resolution.
• Support change and adoption through training materials and hands on guidance for finance users.

What you’ll bring to the table:
• 8+ years’ experience in finance or accounting roles.
• Proven ERP implementation experience, including configuring or supporting Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations.
• Strong understanding of accounting principles, internal controls, compliance and audit requirements.
• Demonstrated experience supporting data migration, system testing and user training during ERP implementations.
• Ability to collaborate effectively with both finance and technical teams, translating business requirements into system solutions.
• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to work closely with finance teams, IT specialists, project managers, vendors and non financial stakeholders.
• Proven capability to bridge the gap between technical teams and finance users to ensure shared understanding and successful outcomes.
• Comprehensive understanding of end to end finance processes, including purchasing, invoicing, revenue recognition and financial close.
• Strong appreciation of how finance processes impact broader business operations.
• Ability to align system configuration with strategic objectives, delivering transparency, insight and business value beyond transactional processing.
• Related degree or CPA/CA
