The Opportunity

An exciting opportunity to join a leading general insurer in Australia and New Zealand, recognised for its strong market position, collaborative culture and investment in data-driven decision making.

This role sits within a high-performing FP&A function and will partner closely with senior leaders across the business to drive financial performance and commercial outcomes. You will also lead a small team of analysts, providing both technical and people leadership.

Key Responsibilities
Partner with senior stakeholders to provide commercial insights and financial advice to support strategic decision making
Lead and develop a team of 1–2 Finance Business Partnering analysts
Deliver detailed insurance performance analysis, including Loss Ratio and NCOR, identifying trends and opportunities to improve profitability
Present financial performance and insights to senior leadership, including recommendations and commentary
Lead budgeting and forecasting across P&L, Balance Sheet and Cash Flow
Support Board and Committee reporting, including performance analysis and competitor insights
Manage legal entity reporting, including transfer pricing and reconciliation between management and statutory results
Improve reporting capability and automation, including greater utilisation of Power BI and FP&A systems
Provide financial modelling and ad hoc analysis to support strategic initiatives and business projects
About You To be considered for this role you must have:

General insurance experience (essential)
Proven people leadership experience, including managing or developing finance analysts
Strong experience analysing Loss Ratio and/or NCOR within an insurance environment
CA / CPA qualification (or equivalent)
Experience in Finance Business Partnering, FP&A, or commercial finance roles
Strong financial modelling, analytical and stakeholder engagement skills
Ability to communicate complex financial insights to senior business stakeholders
Why Apply
Opportunity to work with a top-tier general insurer
High visibility business partnering role with senior stakeholders
Team leadership responsibility
Modern hybrid working environment and strong collaborative culture
Apply now or send your CV to josh@lansonpartners.com to learn more.

About Francom

Financial freedom is the Australian dream. At our core, we exist to help people regain financial control when the system has stopped feeling fair, navigable, or humane. We step into moments of imbalance - where power, information or options are uneven and apply discipline, integrity and judgement to reach outcomes that are sustainable, lawful and human. Join us with your experience as a senior data engineer to apply systems and data thinking that will help restore agency when people need clarity, fairness and a way forward. At Francom our values drive every part of the work we do from ideation, to implementation. Our systems, processes and technology enable our operations.
Our work focuses on resolution over enforcement, and progress over pressure. Financial freedom means enabling people to move forward with dignity. We’re seeking senior data engineers to design and build the data foundations that support real-time analytics, and decision-making.

The Role

We are seeking a Senior Data Engineer to provide technical leadership within our data team. This role is responsible for designing scalable, secure, and reliable data infrastructure while setting best practice standards across data engineering, governance, and delivery.
The Senior Data Engineer will take end-to-end ownership of complex data pipelines and architectures, mentor junior team members, and partner closely with business and technical stakeholders to deliver trusted, high-quality data solutions that support strategic and operational decision-making.

Key responsibilities

Lead the design, build and optimisation of enterprise-grade data pipelines, ELT processes and CI/CD workflows using Azure Data Factory, DBT and Azure Databricks
Architect, build and govern curated and aggregated data layers to support analytics, reporting and regulatory requirements
Develop and optimise complex SQL workloads, ensuring scalability, performance and data integrity
Use Python to support advanced data transformation, orchestration and automation
Build and maintain integrations across data warehouses, real-time streaming platforms, databases, ETL tools, governance platforms and BI tools
Data quality, governance and security

Ensure data integrity across the modern data platform, including accuracy, consistency and completeness
Champion strong data governance, quality, security and documentation practices
Secure data at rest and in transit through appropriate encryption and access controls
Create and publish data flow diagrams, data models and system documentation
Partner with analysts and business users to build and maintain the enterprise data catalogue
Delivery, operations and support

Translate business requirements into robust, compliant and scalable data solutions
Support platform enhancements, capacity planning, performance monitoring and incident resolution
Provide second-level support on a rotational roster
Work closely with the broader team to meet delivery commitments and operational targets
Leadership and collaboration

Provide technical leadership, guidance and mentoring to data engineers and analysts
Drive continuous improvement in engineering practices, delivery quality and team engagement
Act in accordance with Francom’s risk, compliance and values frameworks, setting a high standard for accountability and professional conduct
About You

Tertiary qualification in engineering, mathematics, computer science, or a related discipline.
4-7 years of experience in building and maintaining enterprise grade data infrastructure
Advanced technical capability in SQL, Python, Git, and CI/CD pipelines
Extensive hands-on experience with Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure DevOps, Power BI, and GitHub
Deep understanding of ELT/ETL architectures, data warehousing, and modern data platforms
Proven experience designing and governing aggregated data models in production environments
Experience in transforming and manipulating large and complex datasets. Transactional and financial data and banking/ payments experience highly regarded
Strong problem-solving, decision-making, and stakeholder engagement skills
Experienced with SQL modern data stores, able to write complex queries and transformations.
Demonstrable track record of dealing well with ambiguity, prioritizing needs, and delivering results in a dynamic environment.
Full Australian working rights
Champion a culture of innovation, experimentation, and continuous improvement across the data platform.
Can do attitude
Nice to have:

Familiarity with data reconciliation, PII and PCI concepts for working with payment card and transaction data.
Good understanding of data profiling, query design
Extensive knowledge of cloud scripting (Python) in one or more data processing technologies
Familiar with APIs to pull data from various systems/platforms.
Experience with security, networking, and compliance best practices across the platform.
Support ML pipeline integrations and AI-focused data initiatives.
Software engineering or DevOps experience (e.g. CI/CD, source control, infrastructure as code)
What We Offer

A senior role with real influence in a values-driven organisation
The opportunity to lead, mentor, and shape Francom’s data engineering capability
A professional, collaborative, and accountable culture investing in community impact and positioning for growth
Modern Parramatta office location where some travel may be required.
Free monthly lunches, perks, acknowledgements and birthday celebrations
Competitive salary package aligned to seniority, experience, and contribution
Please note: This role is office-based and is not available on a remote or hybrid basis.

Ad Text:
Vice President (Operations) – 15‑Month Max Term Contract

QIC Private Equity | Brisbane

A rare opportunity to step into a senior operational leadership role within one of Australia’s most respected global investment managers.

QIC’s Private Equity (QPE) team is offering a 15‑month maternity leave contract for a highly capable Vice President focused on Operations. This role is ideal for an investment operations leader ready to take ownership of a global, complex portfolio and drive operational excellence in a fast‑growing asset class.

About the Role

As Vice President, you will oversee the end‑to‑end operational, data and reporting platform, supporting a global private equity program with more than 3,000 underlying companies across funds, co‑investments, secondaries and direct equity investments.

You’ll be the engine room of QPE’s operations, uplifting systems, strengthening governance, elevating data insight, and ensuring reporting and performance narratives exceed client expectations.

You will lead a small, high‑performing team and act as a client‑facing representative of QPE. The role is diverse and hands‑on, with exposure across:

Operational leadership and uplift

eFront platform ownership

Forecasting, reporting and analytics

Valuation assurance and audit oversight

Post‑investment portfolio monitoring

Client reporting and presentations

Partnering with QPE’s global team, and internal QIC teams

This is a max‑term contract, enabling a generous handover period with the incumbent and offering a unique development opportunity in a growing asset class.

About You

In this role, you will draw on a genuine enjoyment of solving complex problems and bringing structure and clarity to fast‑moving environments. 

A background in funds management, particularly within private equity or venture capital operations or fund accounting, combined with strong data and reporting capabilities, will provide a strong foundation. It will be important to stay composed under pressure, work through detail while keeping sight of the bigger picture and offer insights that others can rely on.

You will bring a global outlook and a natural sense of ownership to your work. Because the role involves engaging with sophisticated stakeholders, being comfortable having informed, honest conversations, and explaining complex information in a clear, approachable way, will make a meaningful difference. Reputation is established from consistently delivering thoughtful, accurate, and dependable work.

You will also play a key role in leading and supporting a capable, small hardworking team. An approachable and fair leadership style, with a focus on providing constructive feedback and creating opportunities for growth, will help the team thrive.

This position suits someone who values improving processes, strengthening systems and creating an environment where people can do their best work.

Your Responsibilities

In this role, you will:

Drive Operational Excellence

Lead process optimisation, data uplift and workflow improvements across QPE.

Champion adoption and enhancement of QPE’s core systems, particularly eFront.
 

Own the Data & Reporting Platform

Maintain a trusted, scalable data environment for investment reporting and analysis.

Coordinate quarterly investment reviews and client reporting, ensuring high‑quality, reconciled, mandate‑compliant outputs.
 

Strengthen Governance & Assurance

Support valuations and lead assurance activities.

Drive insight‑rich reporting that enhances transparency and decision‑making.
 

Lead Portfolio Monitoring & Forecasting

Oversee cash forecasting, fee modelling and performance analytics across the portfolio.

Drive comprehensive portfolio‑level monitoring.
 

Provide Senior Client & Stakeholder Engagement

Represent QPE in client meetings and reporting discussions.

Partner with Investment teams, Client Services, Finance, IT, and external providers.
 

Lead & Develop a Team

Manage, mentor and develop two high‑performing analysts.

Essential Criteria

To be successful, you will bring:

Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Finance or Accounting

Postgraduate qualifications such as CA, CFA or CAIA (highly regarded)

Experience in Private Equity or Venture Capital and their fund structures, portfolio monitoring and operational processes

Global experience preferred

Strong operational background, particularly in system enhancement and process re‑engineering

Proven experience partnering with IT and data teams

Client‑facing capability with institutional clients

Exceptional attention to detail, high accountability, resilience and a strong ownership mindset

Experience using eFront or similar private markets platforms

Why Join QIC?

QIC is a global investment manager with over A$100 billion in assets under management. You will join a purpose‑led organisation that delivers shared value for clients, communities and people.

Within QPE, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team working in one of the most exciting private markets portfolios in Australia—with exposure to international offices including San Francisco and Copenhagen.

You’ll also benefit from:

A generous handover period

Working with a leader invested in team development

A high‑performance culture where insight and innovation are valued

Ready to Apply

If you’re a motivated operational leader seeking a rare opportunity to step into a senior role within a global private equity team, we’d love to hear from you.

This is an exceptional chance to continue driving QPE’s operational excellence—while gaining experience across a diversified and high‑growth private markets platform.

Job Title: Principal Business Analyst - 12 Month Max Term Contract
Ad Text:
A great opportunity to work with one of Australia’s largest general insurers 

Lead capability development by mentoring and uplifting AI capability

Open to Sydney candidates only - Hybrid Flexible Opportunity

About Hollard

Hollard Insurance is part of the Hollard Group of Companies and one of largest General Insurer in Australia and New Zealand. We underwrite a broad range of general insurance products, including motor, home, contents, business, bicycle and pet, in proud partnership with some of Australia's biggest consumer brands and broker businesses. As an independent and privately owned company, we believe we can go further together – for our people, customers, communities and partners – through an innovative approach to providing insurance. 

Our state-of-the-art facilities in North Sydney reflect our commitment to providing innovative workspaces that support hybrid and remote work. 

Your Team

You will be part of a multi-disciplinary Technology team, working closely with business, software vendors and partners to develop, implement and maintain systems that support customers through all aspects of the insurance value chain.

Your Role

Reporting to the Business Analysis & Change Practice Manager, you will be the Practice’s technical expert and most senior BA practitioner.

This role is focused on three things: upholding and evolving BA quality standards across the portfolio, leading BA activities during concept phases, and driving the Practice’s AI capability through hands-on tool development.

You will work alongside BAs at all levels — sharing expertise, role-modelling best practice, and contributing to Practice sessions — with people leadership and career development remaining with the Practice Manager

Your duties and responsibilities

Provide technical coaching and skills development to 45 Business Analysts across all capability levels (Junior to Lead BA), focusing on core BA skills including requirements elicitation, stakeholder analysis and engagement, process mapping, user story writing, impact assessment, and effective use of BA tools and templates

Conduct capability assessments to identify skill gaps and develop targeted coaching interventions, training materials, workshops, and knowledge-sharing sessions on BA methodologies, tools, and emerging industry practices.

Co-facilitate BA Practice sessions and uplift programmes to enhance technical capability, share learnings, and embed best practices across the Practice.

Lead the BA Practice’s AI capability uplift by designing and building AI-powered tools, prompt libraries, artefact templates, and workflow automations using Hollard-approved tools (including Claude and Microsoft Copilot). Act as the Practice’s hands-on AI innovator, translating emerging AI capabilities into practical, reusable assets that enhance productivity and quality across the cohort.

Establish and monitor quality gates at key milestones, ensuring adherence to Practice standards and governance across all projects

Collaborate closely with the Business Analysis & Change Practice Manager, EPMO, delivery teams, and business stakeholders to drive successful project outcomes.

About You

Demonstrated experience in delivering Business Analysis activities across the full delivery lifecycle (concept, planning, delivery, implementation), including project scoping, process modelling, and requirements management.

Strong coaching and mentoring skills to technically develop business analysts at various capability levels, with a track record of successfully uplifting BA technical capability.

Must have experience within Agile delivery environments, large enterprise programmes, and technology transformation initiatives

Good Industry/business knowledge of the Australian Insurance market, with demonstrated experience in General Insurance and understanding of APRA regulatory requirements and their implications for business analysis activities

Demonstrated hands-on experience developing AI-powered tools, templates, and workflow automations — not just using AI tools but building with them.

Experience using large language models (such as Claude, ChatGPT, or Copilot) to develop practical BA assets including prompt libraries, AI-assisted artefact templates, and automated BA workflows.

Working at Hollard 

At Hollard, you’ll discover an environment where you can truly make an impact. We offer the best of both worlds – the opportunity, agility and creativity of a company growing with scale, alongside a focus on becoming a catalyst for enduring, positive, social change. 

We’re a tight-knit team, where authentic voices and unique perspectives matter. We work supportively, collaboratively and inclusively to help shape our future and create shared value for our communities. 

Hollard proudly promotes flexible work including remote work, non-traditional work patterns, and work-family balance. We’re also unapologetically committed to creating a workplace that values your diversity with a true sense of inclusion.  We actively seek to hire for your different lived experiences, including as it relates to disability, ethnicity, faith, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, gender diversity, sexual orientation, age, neurodiversity. 

We’re building something special. Working closely with our industry partners to reshape insurance for the better, we’re people changing insurance. 

Please note:  Even if you don’t meet all our selection criteria, we encourage you to apply.  

A full background check will be completed on the successful applicant. 

We will not require any support from Recruitment Agencies and kindly request that no unsolicited resumes are sent to Hollard in relation to this role or others. Only direct applications will be considered.

About Icon Water

Icon Water is an iconic Canberra business with a history of providing essential services to Canberra for over 100 years.

We are the ACT’s supplier of essential water and wastewater services and have proudly served our community for over 100 years – sourcing, treating and supplying water, and managing Canberra’s wastewater network. Our work is essential to the health, prosperity and liveability of the region, and we are committed to environmental, social and financial sustainability in everything we do.

Icon Water is a purpose driven organisation where everyone contributes to our core business, ensuring high quality drinking water and sustainable wastewater treatment. Icon Water’s culture is driven by our values of care, connection, community and courage. These are values we see in action every day as we challenge ourselves to meet the expectations of our community.  




An exciting 6 month opportunity exists to join our People, Health and Safety Group as the HR Systems and Reporting Advisor, where you will be the technical expert and system owner for our Aurion HRMIS core modules and enterprise HR reporting environment. In this pivotal role, you will lead end‑to‑end system administration, configuration, and governance of HR master data and workflows. You will also design, build and deliver high‑quality HR analytics using Power BI.

Key Responsibilities

Oversee the day‑to‑day administration of Aurion HR core modules, including the reporting query tool, organisation structure, position management, establishment control, HR records, and other relevant configuration in partnership with Payroll.
Maintain HR master data to ensure accurate integrations across all Icon Water systems that rely on HR information.
Partner with the IT Applications Support team to manage system upgrades, patches, environment refreshes and release deployments. Coordinate stakeholders, ensure infrastructure readiness, and work with vendors to resolve defects, optimise performance and address service requests.
Use Aurion’s reporting and query tools to set up, maintain and create required reports and dashboards. Build and manage ETL pipelines that ensure accurate and timely data flow into the reporting environment.
Deliver all reporting requirements on time, including commentary and variance analysis, and manage ad hoc reporting requests with documented logic.
Maintain strong reporting governance, including metric definitions, single‑source‑of‑truth frameworks, version control, access controls, and change logs—while driving continuous improvement.
Collaborate with Payroll, Finance, IT and People & Culture teams to align system configuration and reporting with business needs. Provide training and coaching to stakeholders on system usage and dashboard interpretation.

About You

You bring extensive experience using Aurion and hold a relevant degree or equivalent experience in workforce planning, commerce, IT, HR, project management, scheduling, finance or analytics. You also have:

Proven ability to proactively deliver high‑quality, solutions‑focused workforce planning or HR systems support to a diverse client group, including senior managers.
Experience leading organisational change management initiatives.
Strong strategic and holistic thinking, including the ability to anticipate and quantify costs, benefits and impacts of different courses of action.
Ability to integrate people data from multiple HR sources and channels.
Strong relationship‑building skills and the ability to establish credibility with key stakeholders.

To find out more about the role you can view the detailed Position Description on the Icon water Careers Portal.

Ad Text:
About QIC

As one of Australias leading institutional investment managers with more than $100 billion in assets under management, we have a presence across four continents, seven offices and assets across global markets.

At QIC, we believe in shared value - where delivering strong investment returns can go hand in hand with doing good for our clients, people and communities. If we invest well, we will all do better together.

Life at QIC

We know that our people perform at their best when they feel a sense of belonging and are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work every day.  We provide a supportive work environment where everyone can thrive and feel valued for their meaningful contribution in delivering for the prosperity of our clients, people and communities.

Joining QIC, you can contribute to our employee led networks that champion our diversity, equity and inclusion strategy, which consists of five key pillars: Gender Equality, LGBTQ+, Accessibility, Cultural & Linguistic Diversity, and First Nations People.

Other reasons to join us:
Flexibility and hybrid options encouraged – certified Family Inclusive Workplace
Recharge leave - additional 3 days leave per year
Annual Fitness/home office reimbursement
Ability to swap public holidays for those of personal, cultural or religious significance
26 weeks gender-neutral paid parental leave
Financial study assistance and study leave
Community leave for supporting the causes you love
Development opportunities to extend, expand and enhance your growth.
At QIC, we welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, the LGBTQI+ community, people with disabilities and people from all cultural backgrounds. We have an equitable, barrier-free application process so please inform us of your accessibility requirements in your application or you can reach out to our Talent Team on 07 3360 3800.

Vice President (Operations) – 15‑Month Max Term Contract

QIC Private Equity | Brisbane

A rare opportunity to step into a senior operational leadership role within one of Australia’s most respected global investment managers.

QIC’s Private Equity (QPE) team is offering a 15‑month maternity leave contract for a highly capable Vice President focused on Operations. This role is ideal for an investment operations leader ready to take ownership of a global, complex portfolio and drive operational excellence in a fast‑growing asset class.

About the Role

As Vice President, you will oversee the end‑to‑end operational, data and reporting platform, supporting a global private equity program with more than 3,000 underlying companies across funds, co‑investments, secondaries and direct equity investments.

You’ll be the engine room of QPE’s operations, uplifting systems, strengthening governance, elevating data insight, and ensuring reporting and performance narratives exceed client expectations.

You will lead a small, high‑performing team and act as a client‑facing representative of QPE. The role is diverse and hands‑on, with exposure across:

Operational leadership and uplift

eFront platform ownership

Forecasting, reporting and analytics

Valuation assurance and audit oversight

Post‑investment portfolio monitoring

Client reporting and presentations

Partnering with QPE’s global team, and internal QIC teams

This is a max‑term contract, enabling a generous handover period with the incumbent and offering a unique development opportunity in a growing asset class.

About You

In this role, you will draw on a genuine enjoyment of solving complex problems and bringing structure and clarity to fast‑moving environments. 

A background in funds management, particularly within private equity or venture capital operations or fund accounting, combined with strong data and reporting capabilities, will provide a strong foundation. It will be important to stay composed under pressure, work through detail while keeping sight of the bigger picture and offer insights that others can rely on.

You will bring a global outlook and a natural sense of ownership to your work. Because the role involves engaging with sophisticated stakeholders, being comfortable having informed, honest conversations, and explaining complex information in a clear, approachable way, will make a meaningful difference. Reputation is established from consistently delivering thoughtful, accurate, and dependable work.

You will also play a key role in leading and supporting a capable, small hardworking team. An approachable and fair leadership style, with a focus on providing constructive feedback and creating opportunities for growth, will help the team thrive.

This position suits someone who values improving processes, strengthening systems and creating an environment where people can do their best work.

Your Responsibilities

In this role, you will:

Drive Operational Excellence

Lead process optimisation, data uplift and workflow improvements across QPE.

Champion adoption and enhancement of QPE’s core systems, particularly eFront.
 

Own the Data & Reporting Platform

Maintain a trusted, scalable data environment for investment reporting and analysis.

Coordinate quarterly investment reviews and client reporting, ensuring high‑quality, reconciled, mandate‑compliant outputs.
 

Strengthen Governance & Assurance

Support valuations and lead assurance activities.

Drive insight‑rich reporting that enhances transparency and decision‑making.
 

Lead Portfolio Monitoring & Forecasting

Oversee cash forecasting, fee modelling and performance analytics across the portfolio.

Drive comprehensive portfolio‑level monitoring.
 

Provide Senior Client & Stakeholder Engagement

Represent QPE in client meetings and reporting discussions.

Partner with Investment teams, Client Services, Finance, IT, and external providers.
 

Lead & Develop a Team

Manage, mentor and develop two high‑performing analysts.

Essential Criteria

To be successful, you will bring:

Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Finance or Accounting

Postgraduate qualifications such as CA, CFA or CAIA (highly regarded)

Experience in Private Equity or Venture Capital and their fund structures, portfolio monitoring and operational processes

Global experience preferred

Strong operational background, particularly in system enhancement and process re‑engineering

Proven experience partnering with IT and data teams

Client‑facing capability with institutional clients

Exceptional attention to detail, high accountability, resilience and a strong ownership mindset

Experience using eFront or similar private markets platforms

Why Join QIC?

QIC is a global investment manager with over A$100 billion in assets under management. You will join a purpose‑led organisation that delivers shared value for clients, communities and people.

Within QPE, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team working in one of the most exciting private markets portfolios in Australia—with exposure to international offices including San Francisco and Copenhagen.

You’ll also benefit from:

A generous handover period

Working with a leader invested in team development

A high‑performance culture where insight and innovation are valued

Ready to Apply

If you’re a motivated operational leader seeking a rare opportunity to step into a senior role within a global private equity team, we’d love to hear from you.

This is an exceptional chance to continue driving QPE’s operational excellence—while gaining experience across a diversified and high‑growth private markets platform.

About You

Strong writing and reporting skills, with the ability to prepare executive-level reports.
Experience analysing data and translating insights into meaningful information.
Highly organised with the ability to keep projects and workflows on track.
